title: Expose DevMesh runtime status through MCP
summary: DevMesh now provides a mesh_get_status MCP tool so Codex and other MCP clients can verify the running DevMesh version, mode, project root/store, daemon details, and knowledge counts before assuming their configuration is correct. Runtime version reporting is centralized through DEV_MESH_VERSION and release version checks now verify that constant against the root and CLI package versions.

title: Release DevMesh 0.1.2
summary: [done] Released DevMesh 0.1.2 by bumping synchronized workspace/CLI/runtime versions, validating with pnpm release:check, committing c7adff0, tagging v0.1.2, and pushing main plus the tag. GitHub Actions NPM Publish and Release Artifacts both completed successfully; npm dist-tag alpha now points to devmesh@0.1.2 and GitHub prerelease v0.1.2 is published.

title: Website Pages deployment blocked by repository Pages setting
summary: [blocked] Attempted to deploy the DevMesh VitePress website through the Website Pages workflow. The build job and Pages artifact upload succeeded, but the deploy job failed with GitHub Pages deployment 404: Pages must be enabled for xy200303/DevMesh and Source must be set to GitHub Actions. Local environment has no GH_TOKEN/GITHUB_TOKEN, so repository settings cannot be changed programmatically from this session. After enabling Pages in Settings > Pages, rerun Website Pages or push a new main commit.

title: Website docs should use published DevMesh CLI install
summary: DevMesh website docs must avoid local source paths or workspace-only commands and should present the published npm CLI flow for users.
content: Updated website docs to remove leaked local Windows paths/source workspace commands and document the install-first flow: `npm install -g devmesh@alpha`, `dmx init`, `dmx status`, `dmx doctor`, `dmx capture`, `dmx search`, and optional team sync via `dmx join`. Verify future website edits with a leak scan for local path tokens and source-dev command examples before publishing.

title: Redesign website homepage and complete interface reference
summary: [done] Redesigned the DevMesh website homepage into a plain docs-style entry and added complete CLI, MCP, and HTTP API reference documentation. Pushed commit 8855e9c.
content: Updated the website homepage to remove the poster-like centered hero and use a restrained left-aligned docs entry with install command, common commands, command groups, and interface links. Expanded `reference/cli.md` into a full command and option reference. Added `reference/mcp.md` covering all 14 MCP tools and input fields. Added `reference/http.md` covering Hub discovery, join, sync, project, admin, and `/mcp` endpoints. Updated VitePress sidebar navigation. Verified with `pnpm build:website`, `git diff --check`, hidden leak scan for local paths/dev commands, Playwright homepage screenshot at 1440x900, and pushed commit 8855e9c. `.dev-mesh/` remains untracked local runtime data.

Verified with `pnpm build:website`, `git diff --check`, a hidden leak scan over website docs/dist, and a search confirming the command appears in the built homepage. `.dev-mesh/` remains untracked local runtime data.

title: Website docs should use published DevMesh CLI install
summary: DevMesh website docs must avoid local source paths or workspace-only commands and should present the published npm CLI flow for users.
content: Updated website docs to remove leaked local Windows paths/source workspace commands and document the install-first flow: `npm install -g devmesh@alpha`, `dmx init`, `dmx status`, `dmx doctor`, `dmx capture`, `dmx search`, and optional team sync via `dmx join`. Verify future website edits with a leak scan for local path tokens and source-dev command examples before publishing.

title: Improve DevMesh website SEO and homepage polish
summary: [done] [done] Added VitePress SEO metadata, sitemap config, robots.txt, OG preview assets, and refined the homepage with a cleaner hero, terminal preview, setup flow, command/API entry cards, and responsive styling. Verified build, sitemap/robots/meta output, leak scan, desktop/mobile screenshots, then committed and pushed 3d4797c.

title: Expose DevMesh runtime status through MCP
summary: DevMesh now provides a mesh_get_status MCP tool so Codex and other MCP clients can verify the running DevMesh version, mode, project root/store, daemon details, and knowledge counts before assuming their configuration is correct. Runtime version reporting is centralized through DEV_MESH_VERSION and release version checks now verify that constant against the root and CLI package versions.

title: Add knowledge CRUD MCP tools
summary: [done] [done] Added explicit get/list/update/delete MCP tools for knowledge entries, wired them through core, local-store events, runtime, local stdio/HTTP proxy, server MCP, and tests. Verification passed for package typechecks, builds, and focused vitest suites. Committed as 0f38b86.

title: DevMesh 0.1.3 alpha published
summary: DevMesh v0.1.3 was released to npm with the alpha dist-tag and tagged as v0.1.3 on GitHub.
content: Release commit 64647f6 prepares DevMesh 0.1.3 by synchronizing package.json, apps/dmx/package.json, and DEV_MESH_VERSION, refreshing the README hero into a centered badge layout, and updating the shared SVG logo. Verification passed with pnpm build:website, git diff --check, and pnpm release:check. The v0.1.3 tag was pushed to GitHub and npm confirmed devmesh@0.1.3 with alpha pointing to 0.1.3.

title: README should stay product-facing and bilingual
summary: DevMesh root README should be a concise product entrypoint, with README.md in Chinese and README-EN.md in English, while detailed CLI/API/development material lives in website docs, CONTRIBUTING.md, and release docs.
content: Commit b9897c3 rewrote the root README into a product-facing overview: install command, local-first value, assistant-led MCP capture model, shareable .dev-mesh knowledge files, core commands, interface links, and security notes. It added README-EN.md with parallel English content, CONTRIBUTING.md for development and knowledge-store policy, and .github/pull_request_template.md for PR expectations. Avoid reintroducing long internal implementation dumps into README; link to devmesh.xyun.dev, docs/release.md, and CONTRIBUTING.md instead.
